title: Track uses of FIXME tags
description: Track uses of "FIXME" tagsAnalyze your codeintentionality-completemaintainabilityCode SmellcweWhy is this an issue?More InfoFIXMEtags are commonly used to mark places where a bug is suspected, but which the developer wants to deal with later.Sometimes the developer will not have the time or will simply forget to get back to that tag.This rule is meant to track those tags and to ensure that they do not go unnoticed.private int Divide(int numerator, int denominator) { return numerator / denominator; // FIXME denominator value might be 0 }Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIAvailable Since9.1Analyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: FIXME tag found - this indicates a suspected bug that should be addressed.

never_trigger_on: FIXME in string literals (pattern only matches comments)
logic.reasoning: FIXME tags are commonly used to mark places where a bug is suspected but the developer wants to deal with it later. This rule tracks those tags to ensure they are addressed and don't go unnoticed. The pattern only matches FIXME in comments starting with // or /*.
